facts.text: Brooklyn Heights was laid out by Hezekiah Pierrepont in the early 19th century, who sold plots to developers after surveying the land in 1802.
The neighborhood became a fashionable residential area after ferry service to Manhattan began in 1814, making it a desirable escape from lowland Brooklyn.
In 1965, community groups succeeded in having Brooklyn Heights designated the city’s first historic district by the Landmarks Preservation Commission.
The Brooklyn Heights Promenade, a cantilevered esplanade over the BQE, was created in 1910 with the help of the Brooklyn Heights Association and Moses.
The first library in Brooklyn Heights opened in 1857, founded by the Mercantile Library Association of the City of Brooklyn.
The neighborhood’s streets often honor early developers and figures, such as Montague Street named for Lady Mary Wortley Montagu and Hicks Street for ferry operators.
